name: Esglesietes de Ponent Trail
name_original: Collado de Sa Creu, subida a Galilea y Coll des Tords — circular por la Sierra Tramuntana
description: Challenge yourself on the Esglesietes de Ponent Trail, a difficult 32.4-mile road cycling route with 3240 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Road cycling around Bosc de Bellver, a national forest in Palma, Mallorca, offers routes that combine natural beauty with historical landmarks. The area features dense vegetation, including towering pine trees, and elevated points that provide panoramic vistas of Palma city, its harbor, and the Mediterranean Sea. While the forest itself contains varied terrain, it serves as an accessible green oasis within Palma, connecting to the wider road cycling network of Mallorca. Best road cycling routes around Bosc de Bellver The most popular road cycling route is Esglesietes de Ponent Trail, a 32.4 miles (52.1 km) difficult trail that takes 2 hours 44 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Puigpunyent village – Coll des Vent loop from sa Teulera, a moderate 23.1 miles (37.1 km) path. This loop offers a journey through villages and over hills, providing a good workout. Local road cyclists also love the Palmanova – Es Carregador Beach loop from Bellver Castle, a 14.4 miles (23.1 km) trail leading through coastal areas and past Bellver Castle, often completed in about 1 hour 7 minutes. Road cycling around Bosc de Bellver is defined by dense woodlands, elevated viewpoints, and connections to coastal routes, with options for different ability levels. The routes in Bosc de Bellver are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 1900 reviews. More than 69,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Bosc de Bellver's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain and elevation can I expect when road cycling around Bosc de Bellver?
A: Road cycling around Bosc de Bellver offers a varied experience. The forest itself features lush, dense vegetation and elevated points, meaning routes will involve climbs, providing a good workout. You'll find a mix of well-maintained roads and, in some areas, wider gravel pathways that might be suitable for hybrid bikes. The elevated positions reward cyclists with panoramic vistas of Palma city, its harbor, and the Mediterranean Sea.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Bosc de Bellver?
A: Yes, Bosc de Bellver offers several easy road cycling routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39544500" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palmanova – Es Carregador Beach loop from Bellver Castle&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 14.4-mile (23.1 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 7 minutes, leading through coastal areas. Another easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39559346" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palma – NANO bicycles® loop from Bellver Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which is a shorter 9.4-mile (15.1 km) route.

Q: Where can I find more challenging road cycling routes in the area?
A: For more challenging rides, consider routes that venture further into the surrounding areas of Mallorca. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1003923567/esglesietes-de-ponent-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Esglesietes de Ponent Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 32.4-mile (52.1 km) route with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1003936045/southern-serra-de-tramuntana-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Southern Serra de Tramuntana Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is over 60 miles (100 km) long and features substantial climbs, offering a true test for advanced cyclists.

Q: What scenic viewpoints can I enjoy while cycling around Bosc de Bellver?
A: Bosc de Bellver is renowned for its elevated points that provide stunning panoramic vistas. As you cycle, you'll be treated to views of Palma city, its bustling harbor, and the expansive Mediterranean Sea. The iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/8075216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bellver Castle&lt;/a&gt; itself offers some of the best viewpoints in the area.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks to see along the road cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The most prominent historical landmark is the 14th-century circular Gothic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/8075216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bellver Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which is central to Bosc de Bellver. It not only offers architectural interest but also houses the city's history museum. Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/760396" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chapel of Saint Alonso Rodríguez&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3977222" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrance to Bosc de Bellver&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is there parking available for cyclists near Bosc de Bellver?
A: Given its urban setting within Palma, there are various parking options around Bosc de Bellver. While specific dedicated cycling parking isn't detailed, general public parking facilities in Palma and near the forest entrances would be available. It's advisable to check local parking regulations and availability, especially during peak seasons.

Q: How can I reach Bosc de Bellver using public transport?
A: Bosc de Bellver's proximity to Palma makes it easily accessible by public transport. Local bus services in Palma typically have stops near the entrances to the forest. This allows cyclists to reach the starting points of routes conveniently without needing a car.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Bosc de Bellver?
A: Mallorca, including Bosc de Bellver, is generally excellent for road cycling outside of the hottest summer months. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, less crowded routes, and beautiful scenery with blooming flowers or vibrant fall foliage. Even winter can be suitable, especially for those escaping colder climates, though some routes might be affected by rain.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes around Bosc de Bellver are designed as loops, offering convenient circular journeys. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39566431" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puigpunyent village – Coll des Vent loop from sa Teulera&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 23.1-mile (37.1 km) path that takes you through villages and over hills.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Bosc de Bellver?
A: The komoot community highly rates road cycling in Bosc de Bellver, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 1900 reviews. Reviewers often praise the unique blend of natural beauty, historical significance, and the rewarding views of Palma and the Mediterranean Sea. The serene atmosphere, dense woodlands, and the feeling of being immersed in nature within an urban setting are frequently highlighted.

Q: Are the road cycling routes in Bosc de Bellver suitable for families?
A: While Bosc de Bellver offers varied terrain, some of the easier and shorter loops, particularly those on well-maintained paths, can be suitable for families with older children or those comfortable with some gentle climbs. The forest's shaded areas and scenic views make for an enjoyable outing. Always check the route difficulty and elevation profile to ensure it matches your family's cycling ability.

Q: Can I find cafes or rest stops along the road cycling routes?
A: Given Bosc de Bellver's location within Palma and its popularity, you'll find various amenities nearby. While the forest itself is a natural oasis, the surrounding areas of Palma offer numerous cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can refuel and rest. Many routes connect to or pass through urban areas where such facilities are readily available.
tips: This is a delightful climb: six kilometers at an average gradient of 5.4%, with 9% gradients, adding 300 meters of elevation gain. Good road surface, easy curves, light traffic, and beautiful views.
Nice cafés in the middle of Calvia on the newly designed square. It's worth taking a break.

Great climb and descent from both sides but it's especially narrow when descending towards the Andratx side so it's worth taking care on the many blind corners. The surface of this climb isn't as good as many Mallorca's roads either, having said that it's absolutely beautiful! Personally I prefer to ride this climb from Es Capdella towards Puigpynyent.

Pleasure trail from Coll des Tords down to the MA 10, branching off to Puigpunyent or Establiments. A nice flowing descent, moderate in the ascent. Very little traffic.
From the Coll des Tords pass you have a good view of the high peaks of the Tramuntana mountains. Even the Puig Major can be seen.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Esglesietes de Ponent Trail?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily designed for road bikes, featuring mostly well-paved surfaces. However, be aware that some segments might be unpaved and challenging, potentially requiring you to dismount and push your bike. Expect significant elevation changes with long ascents and descents, winding roads, and numerous bends as you traverse mountain passes.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: This trail offers captivating rural mountain landscapes and breathtaking views over valleys. You'll pass through charming, hidden villages and encounter several notable mountain passes. Key highlights include the Ascent to Coll de sa Creu from Palma, the historic Church of Saint John the Baptist of Calvià, the challenging Ascent and descent of the Galilea pass, and the scenic Coll des Tords Road.

faqs.question: Is the Esglesietes de Ponent Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating, significant elevation gain of nearly 1,000 meters, and a distance of over 52 kilometers, this trail is best suited for experienced road cyclists with a very good fitness level. Beginners might find the long ascents and challenging sections quite demanding.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: While specific weather details aren't provided, routes in this region are generally enjoyable during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring cooler, potentially wetter conditions, especially at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route overlaps with several other paths. You'll find yourself on sections of the Esglesietes de Ponent, [CIMA IB14] Galilea, GR 221 Dry Stone Route: Variant 2 Galatzó: stretch Calvià-Estellencs, El Camí IT4TR4 El Camí Illenc: de l'Alguer a Formentera, and Llogarets de Ponent.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in Serra de Tramuntana?
faqs.answer: As this trail is located within the Serra de Tramuntana, a UNESCO World Heritage site, it's generally open for cycling without specific permits for individual routes. However, always check local regulations for any protected areas or specific events that might require special permissions or have temporary restrictions. There are typically no entrance fees for cycling on public roads within the park.

faqs.question: Where does the Esglesietes de Ponent Trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The tour description indicates the route includes the Ascent to Coll de sa Creu from Palma, suggesting a potential starting point near Palma. While specific parking details aren't provided, Palma and surrounding towns offer various parking options. For a less crowded experience, consider starting early in the morning, especially if beginning in or near a town.

faqs.question: Is the Esglesietes de Ponent Trail dog-friendly for cycling?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for road cycling, which generally isn't suitable for dogs to accompany cyclists, especially given the 'difficult' rating and significant distance. If you wish to bring a dog, it would likely need to be in a specialized bike trailer, but this is not a typical dog-friendly trail for them to run alongside.

faqs.question: What kind of experience can I expect beyond the cycling itself?
faqs.answer: Beyond the challenging climbs and rewarding descents, this route offers an authentic rural experience. You'll connect with towns largely untouched by mass tourism, allowing you to immerse yourself in the local culture. The region is also known for its local gastronomy and wines, providing opportunities to explore these aspects in the villages you pass through.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Esglesietes de Ponent Trail?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this 52.1-kilometer racebike tour is approximately 2 hours and 44 minutes of active cycling time. However, this can vary significantly based on your fitness level, the number of stops you make for photos or breaks, and weather conditions.

faqs.question: Are there places to find food, drink, or accommodation along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail passes through charming villages, which are likely to offer opportunities for local gastronomy, cafes, and potentially accommodation. These towns, often untouched by mass tourism, provide a chance to experience authentic local offerings. Planning your stops in advance is recommended.
